text: 1:06 The Game Awards thing didn't ruin their shadowdrop plan; their shadowdrop plan was doomed to fail from the start. Not only was it entitled and arrogant (both a result of the toxic positivity) to assume people would instantly love the game, but not getting any real feedback from potential players during development meant they couldn't adjust the game to make it more appealing and fun. E.g. if they were still in development and got the feedback that the game looked like a subpar, generic mashup of the hero shooter genre, that 3v3 as the sole game mode (let alone the main one) at launch was a bad idea, and trying to force competition instead of including it just scares away the casual base needed to develop and support a competitive scene, they could have fixed that and had a chance.
